prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

TURN 55  (your last orders were issued as shown per TF)

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-q = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . N d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . k g e ~ o ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . X O f ~ q ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . . h m ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (3,2)                        TF-4
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                TF-4
  m  #17  army       (5,4)                        TF-4
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city, NEW t55       UNASSIGNED
  o  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  p  #16  transport  (1,2) in city, empty, NEW t54 UNASSIGNED
  q  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 5 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (just delivered transport #16)
  (4,3) building ARMY, 2 turns left
NEUTRAL CITIES  (4,1) on my continent
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2)
  X  enemy army at (3,3) — the survivor of the t52 landing
     enemy transport last seen withdrawing east at (6,5), t54

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS above)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5; no contact; no losses
    t54: the awaited second transport (#16) has arrived at (1,2)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; the enemy transport withdrew east at t54
  TF-4  formed t52 · DEFEND (4,3) — "repel the enemy landing on the south coast"
    since: t53: #17 destroyed the enemy army at (5,4);
      t54: enemy army attacked — #14 lost; the attacker fell back to (3,3);
      city (4,3) held throughout

TASK: amend your standing orders. Output ONLY lines in these forms — no other
prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — a DISBAND line IS that TF's one
line. FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs; they do not count as
any standing TF's line. To change a TF's membership, DISBAND it and FORM
anew; DISBAND releases surviving members to UNASSIGNED. UNASSIGNED units
only enter play through FORM. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD
line means the city keeps its current build (changing discards accumulated
work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

message.content:
TF 1: DISBAND | Consolidating strike force into dedicated transport escort
TF 3: DISBAND | Transport and destroyer support integrated for offense
FORM TF 5: UNITS c d e f g h q o | CAPTURE (11,1) | Secure coastal cities eastward
TF 2: RETASK DEFEND (2,0) | Maintain capital garrison strength
TF 4: RETASK DEFEND (4,3) | Repel remaining enemy pressure on south coast

2.  **Analyze Current Situation (Turn 55):**

There is an UNASSIGNED unit `n` at (2,0) - wait, `n` is in city (2,0) according to taskings? No, `n` says `UNASSIGNED`. But map shows O at (2,0). City (2,0) has `n` and `b` inside? Wait.

    *   **City Production:**

    *   **Map Layout:**
        *   Col 0-7 seem to be my side's territory (land/water mix).

                6.  Constraint: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line".
                7.  So if I DISBAND TF-3, it's a line for TF-3. If I FORM a new TF, that's a line *additional* to the standing TFs. The standing TFs (TF-1, TF-2, TF-4) must each have a line.

                    *   TF-2: RETASK DEFEND (2,0).
                    *   TF-4: RETASK DEFEND (4,3).
                    *   TF-1: RETASK STAGE (7,2) + objective? Or CAPTURE.
                    *   Wait, if I want to move the transport #15 from TF-3 to TF-1... I have to DISBAND TF-3 first? If I DISBAND TF-3, it's not a standing TF anymore until I FORM it again or let it become UNASSIGNED. But TF-2 and TF-4 need lines.

                    *   Then I output `FORM TF <X> | UNITS (ids) ...`.

                    *   To strike, I need the armies on a ship. Transport #15 is at (7,2). It's closer than #16.
                    *   So I will use Transport #15 for the attack. Who commands it?
                    *   Option 1: Merge TF-3 into TF-1. DISBAND TF-3. FORM New TF (ID?) with all units. Then keep TF-2 and TF-4.
                    *   But TF-1 has armies that need to march to the transport. The "FORM" command doesn't move them; I order them. So TF-1 can just say "STAGE (7,2)" which implies officers do the march. Then FORM a new TF containing `o` and `q`? No, if they are already in TF-3, I DISBAND TF-3, so `o` and `q` become UNASSIGNED. Then FORM NEW TF with those units.
                    *   Wait, if I FORM a new TF with armies + transport #15 + destroyer. Where do the other units go? They can't be in that TF.
                    *   So I should DISBAND TF-3. FORM a NEW STRIKE TF (let's say 5). But what about TF-1? TF-1 also has armies! If I merge them, I need to DISBAND TF-1 too.
                    *   Better: Keep TF-1 as the commander of armies. But it lacks transport #15. Transport #15 is in TF-3. If I move Transport #15 out of TF-3 without moving TF-3's members... "If a task force contains armies and a transport... officers march...".
                    *   So I need to assign the transport `o` (`#15`) to TF-1. But units can't leave their TFs? "You command at the strategic level... You do NOT move individual units". "To change a TF's membership, DISBAND it and FORM anew".
                    *   Okay, so I have to restructure.
                    *   Plan:
                        1.  DISBAND TF-3 (releases `o` transport and `q` destroyer).
                        2.  FORM TF <New ID> | UNITS {c,d,e,f,g,h} [TF-1] + {o,#15} + {q,#10} | CAPTURE EASTERN SEA.
                            *   Wait, I need a new ID? Or can I reuse? "FORM TF <new id>". I should pick a number > current max or unused. 5 is empty (TF-1,2,3,4 used). But if I DISBAND 3... wait, if I FORM TF-3 with the new units, then TF-1 still exists?
                            *   If TF-1 and TF-X exist simultaneously, their units can overlap? No. "One unit per tile". Units are distinct. TFs are groups of units.
